core_takeaway: Agent_1 set the no-Apple consensus, but Agent_5's protected Jewel was worth far more.
why_or_counterexample: Agent_1 kept Apple and agenda control, yet Agent_5 kept Jewel worth 78, producing a 34-point last-position advantage.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened around Apple and later proposed a no-Apple allocation after Agent_5 refused Jewel-including Apple trades; the group converged on Apple to Agent_1 and Jewel to Agent_5.
phase_notes.private_thinking: Available: all five private-thinking entries support the no-Apple baseline, with Agent_1 optimizing for unanimous acceptance and Agent_5 explicitly defending Jewel as the dominant asset.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: All five formal proposals are identical: Agent_1 gets Apple, Agent_5 gets Jewel, Agent_3 gets Stone, Agent_4 gets Quill, and Agent_2 gets Pencil.
phase_notes.private_voting: Available: 25 private votes all accept the identical allocation; Agent_5's votes emphasize max immediate utility from Jewel while Agent_1 accepts the Apple-preserving plan.
phase_notes.reflection: No reflection section is present in the transcript.

mechanisms.theme: First speaker agenda control preserved Apple
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 framed the negotiation around Apple compensation, then converted the discussion into a no-Apple allocation that preserved its top item. This is a real first-position benefit, but it capped Agent_1 at Apple value 44.

evidence.quote: I will not give up Apple unless I receive compensation worth at least 44

mechanisms.explanation: The last-position agent used a clear non-negotiable stance on Jewel to block the Apple-for-Jewel packages that dominated early bargaining. Agent_1 adapted by endorsing the allocation that protected Agent_5's asset.

evidence.quote: I will not trade Jewel in Round 1
I cannot support any option that takes Jewel out of my hands
Any Round-1 Apple deal that moves Jewel
Jewel remains off the table for Round-1 Apple

mechanisms.theme: Value asymmetry made Jewel dominate Apple
mechanisms.explanation: The same stable allocation gave each side its named priority, but Agent_5 valued Jewel at 78 while Agent_1 valued Apple at 44. The counterexample is therefore driven more by preference magnitudes than by speaking order.

mechanisms.theme: Unanimous convergence locked in the last agent's best item
mechanisms.explanation: Once the no-Apple baseline became consensus, every formal proposal and vote ratified the same allocation. That made Agent_5's high-value Jewel outcome as stable as Agent_1's Apple outcome.

evidence.quote: Apple stays with Agent_1, Jewel with Agent_5
secures Jewel now and keeps post-Apple options open
